alternatives: Mandate Swift Testing for every test capability
Mandate XCTest for all new and existing tests
Require TDD and runtime gates for documentation and metadata
Accept AI assertions or generated green output as executable test evidence
consequences: Framework choice follows required capability and repository compatibility
TDD effort follows executable behavior and regression risk
Asynchronous tests use deterministic bounded signals
Documentation consistency relies on the final joined AI review while executable-code claims remain command-backed
Project-owned tests use behavior-oriented camelCase naming
context: iOS repositories need deterministic confidence across several test tiers without forcing one framework or fabricating runtime evidence for non-executable documentation.
decision: Choose Swift Testing or XCTest by capability and repository compatibility; apply TDD only to executable behavior proportionally to risk; require deterministic bounded asynchronous tests, risk-based unit through performance signals, observed command-backed evidence for executable-code claims, final joined AI review for documentation consistency, and camelCase testScenarioExpectation naming.
migration: Adopt the framework boundary for new work, replace timing sleeps with controlled asynchronous dependencies, rename touched project-owned tests to testScenarioExpectation, remove tests that cannot falsify behavior, and route documentation consistency to final AI review.
title: Testing and evidence boundary
verification_impact: The final joined AI consistency review confirms the chapter, Rule records, framework boundary, executable-only TDD scope, evidence hierarchy, and camelCase naming remain aligned.
